To design a machine that can be used to hack into people's minds and extract information, we would need to consider several key factors. Firstly, the machine would need to have the ability to non-invasively read brain activity, as this is currently the only way to access someone's thoughts without their consent.

One approach to building such a machine could be to use functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) technology. fMRI machines are used in hospitals and research facilities to measure brain activity by detecting changes in blood flow. By placing an individual inside the machine, we can use the fMRI data to map out areas of the brain that are active during different thoughts or activities.
